title: XOR single-number: find the lone element in O(1) space
statement_informal: In a list where one value appears once and every other value appears in pairs, a single left-to-right XOR fold recovers the lone value, in O(1) extra space. Relies on XOR being commutative/associative (order-independent, proved as Permutation-invariance) and self-cancelling (x XOR x = 0).

cost_model.notes: xorsum = fold_right Nat.lxor 0. One pass, O(1) space. Correctness via xorsum_perm (Permutation invariance from lxor comm/assoc) and xorsum_dup (l++l cancels to 0).

references: Folklore bit-trick / common interview problem ('Single Number'): XOR all elements; pairs cancel, the unique one remains.
The XOR analogue of the Boyer-Moore one-pass O(1)-space idea (work-units/boyer-moore-majority).
